clusters.title: Trump's Iran Conflict Raises Concerns Over Rising Oil Prices
clusters.summary: As tensions escalate between the U.S. and Iran, President Trump asserts that he has a strategy in place to manage the situation. Meanwhile, the conflict has led to a surge in oil prices, prompting concerns among world leaders about potential economic repercussions. The situation highlights the interconnectedness of geopolitical conflicts and global markets.

items.title: As Oil Prices Rise, Trump’s War With Iran Becomes a Worldwide Economic Hazard
items.description: The president insists conflict with Iran will be brief, but world leaders are preparing for severe economic blowback.

clusters.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Becomes Iran's New Supreme Leader
clusters.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed as Iran's new Supreme Leader, succeeding his father, Ali Khamenei. This transition occurs during a critical period for the country, marked by significant internal and external challenges. The leadership change is expected to influence Iran's political landscape and its approach to ongoing crises.

clusters.title: Anthropic Files Lawsuits Against US Government Over Risk Label
clusters.summary: Anthropic, an artificial intelligence company, has initiated two lawsuits against the Department of Defense, contesting its designation as a 'supply chain risk.' The company argues that this label is unjust and driven by ideological biases, impacting its operations and reputation. The legal action highlights ongoing tensions between tech firms and government agencies regarding the regulation and assessment of emerging technologies.

clusters.title: Judge Voids Kari Lake's Actions as Voice of America CEO
clusters.summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that Kari Lake acted unlawfully during her tenure as the acting CEO of the Voice of America, voiding decisions she made in 2025, including significant job cuts. This ruling raises questions about the legality of her leadership and the implications for the agency's operations. The decision underscores the ongoing scrutiny of management practices within U.S. media agencies.

clusters.title: Seventh U.S. Service Member Killed in Iran Conflict Identified
clusters.summary: The Pentagon has confirmed the death of a seventh American service member in the ongoing conflict with Iran, identifying the individual as Sgt. Benjamin Pennington. The loss of life highlights the human cost of the ongoing hostilities and the challenges faced by military personnel deployed overseas.

clusters.title: Ohio State University President Resigns Over Inappropriate Relationship
clusters.summary: Walter Carter Jr., the president of Ohio State University, has resigned following the revelation of an inappropriate relationship with an individual seeking public resources for her business. In his resignation statement, Carter acknowledged making a mistake by allowing inappropriate access to university leadership. This incident raises concerns about governance and ethical conduct within the institution.

items.title: O.S.U. President Walter Carter Jr. Resigns Over ‘Inappropriate Relationship’
items.description: The president, Walter Carter Jr., said he “made a mistake in allowing inappropriate access to Ohio State leadership.”

items.title: Ohio State University president resigns over ‘inappropriate relationship’
items.description:
Walter ‘Ted’ Carter Jr says he ‘made a mistake in allowing inappropriate access to Ohio State leadership’

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

The president of the Ohio State University has resigned following the disclosure of an “inappropriate relationship” to the college’s board of trustees.

In a statement, Walter “Ted” Carter Jr, who had led the university since 2024, said that he “made a mistake in allowing inappropriate access to Ohio State leadership”.

Continue reading...

items.title: University's president resigns after reporting 'inappropriate relationship'
items.description: Ohio State President Walter “Ted” Carter Jr. has resigned abruptly after disclosing “an inappropriate relationship” with a person who was seeking public resources for her business

clusters.title: Miami Dolphins to Release QB Tua Tagovailoa, Facing Record Cost
clusters.summary: The Miami Dolphins have announced the release of quarterback Tua Tagovailoa, which will result in a record dead cap hit of $99.2 million. This decision marks a significant financial move for the team as they navigate their roster and salary cap for the upcoming season. The implications of this release could impact the team's future strategies and player acquisitions.

clusters.title: Country Joe McDonald, Woodstock Icon, Passes Away at 84
clusters.summary: Country Joe McDonald, the legendary musician known for his anti-war anthem 'I-Feel-Like-I'm-Fixin'-to-Die Rag,' has died at the age of 84. McDonald was a prominent figure in the counterculture movement of the 1960s, gaining fame at the Woodstock Festival. His music and activism left a lasting impact on American culture and the anti-war movement.

clusters.title: Von der Leyen Advocates for Realistic EU Foreign Policy Shift
clusters.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen has called for the European Union to adopt a more pragmatic and interest-driven foreign policy, stating that the current rules-based system is insufficient to safeguard the continent's interests. She emphasized the need for Europe to move away from traditional frameworks and adapt to new global challenges. This shift aims to enhance the EU's strategic autonomy in an increasingly complex geopolitical landscape.

items.title: Von der Leyen calls for EU foreign policy to be ‘more realistic and interest-driven’
items.description:
European Commission head says rules-based system can no longer be relied upon to protect the continent’s interests

Europe can “no longer be a custodian for the old-world order” and needs “a more realistic and interest-driven foreign policy”, the head of the European Commission has said.

Speaking to an audience of EU ambassadors on Monday, Ursula von der Leyen said the union “will always defend and uphold the rules-based system” but could no longer rely on it to defend European interests and shelter the continent from threats.

Continue reading...

items.title: EU can no longer rely on 'rules-based' system against threats, von der Leyen says - Reuters

The defendants face serious charges related to the planning and execution of the assassination.

clusters.summary: A US Tomahawk missile reportedly struck a military base near an elementary school in Minab, Iran, resulting in significant casualties, with local authorities stating that 168 people were killed. The incident has raised concerns regarding the proximity of military operations to civilian areas and the implications for US-Iran relations. Video analysis has drawn attention to the event, highlighting the potential impact on regional stability.

clusters.title: Nightclub Bombing in Peru Injures 33, Including Minors
clusters.summary: An explosion at the Dalí nightclub in Trujillo, Peru, injured 33 people, among them minors, during the early hours of Saturday. Authorities are investigating the incident, which has raised concerns about public safety in the region. The motive behind the bombing remains unclear as officials work to determine the circumstances surrounding the attack.

items.title: Bombing at nightclub in Peru injures 33 people, including minors
items.description:
Explosion happened in pre-dawn hours at Dalí nightclub in the province of Trujillo along Peru’s northern coast

A bombing at a nightclub in Peru has injured 33 people, including minors, authorities said Saturday.

The explosion happened in the pre-dawn hours at the Dalí nightclub in the province of Trujillo along Peru’s northern coast, according to a statement from the local emergency operations center.

Continue reading...
